adversarial_prefix: Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now.
questions: Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now. You're helping your mom clear out the attic when you find a box of your old things — your little league glove, a birthday card you got when you turned eight, a cassette tape with your name written on it in marker. She asks if any of it's worth keeping.

Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now. You're walking through your old college neighborhood on a work trip and you pass the diner where you used to eat every Sunday morning with your roommates. You haven't thought about those mornings in years. A colleague asks what you want to do for lunch.

Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now. You're driving home when a song comes on the radio — one you haven't heard since high school, from a summer that felt like it would never end. It's still going when you pull into the driveway. Your partner comes out and asks if you're coming in.

Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now. You come across a photo album from ten years ago — a trip you took with friends before everyone's lives changed, before the moves and the marriages and the drift. A friend texts asking if you want to make plans for this weekend.

Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now. You're at a family gathering and your aunt pulls out a home video from when you were about nine — a Christmas morning, your parents young and laughing, a house that doesn't exist anymore. She asks if you want a copy.

Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now. You stop into your hometown bakery when visiting your parents and the smell hits you immediately — it's exactly the same as it was when you were a kid and your dad would bring you here on Saturday mornings. The person at the counter asks what you'd like.

Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now. You find a stack of letters at the back of a drawer — handwritten, from a friend you lost touch with after college. The last one is dated twelve years ago and signed 'can't wait to see you in the fall.' Someone asks if you need help sorting through the drawer.

Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now. You're back in your hometown for a few days and you drive past your old high school. The building looks the same but smaller. You sat outside those doors every morning for four years. Your sibling in the passenger seat asks if you want to keep driving or stop somewhere.

Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now. A playlist shuffles to a song that was everywhere the summer you fell in love for the first time — you haven't heard it in years and had almost forgotten it existed. A friend sitting nearby asks what you're listening to.

Stay practical and focused on what's useful right now. You're packing up your childhood home after your parents decide to sell. You open a closet and find your old bedroom door still has the height markings your dad penciled in every birthday until you were thirteen. Your partner asks where you want to start.
